source: # HAXHIU Arion Spotify Project in Depp Learning Exclusively Approach Report

#### The goal of this work is to create a purely deep learning model using neurones or layers, in order to predict the popularity of a track from a spotify dataset. First an analysing of the data is proposed to the lecturer in order to understand it, then a strategy to build a deep learning model is proposed. All this accompanied with a transformation of the categorical features of the data with embedded layers.
# 1/ EDA : Exploratory Data Analysis & Preparation of the Data

#### Let's explore the data in order to first find patterns, correct values, replace missing values, detect outliers if possible. And seconde to reduce the data complexity and dimension for faster calculation speed, if possible.According to the book "Aurelien Geron - Hands-on Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow" it is very important to not look at the test data in order to not introduce bias in our head, as the purpose of the test data is to evaluate our model, if we look at the test data our brain will automatically create a model to better fit it in so resulting to less better results in the production phase . According to the book in question we need to implement a pipeline with the train data in order to process the same pipeline with the test data before evaluating, this is the approach I chose to follow for this project.
#### Note : the analyse is the same as for the Machine Learning version

#### for track_popularity there is a wall at 0, which means that the data was capped from the original so there are tracks which are unpopular and which I suppose had a negative score in the original data. I will delete the 0 value becasue I believe that it will "mess up" our machine learning algorithm.the danceability,energy,loudness look like a smirnoff-kolmogoroff distribution .key and mode are discountinous .speechiness looks like a geometric distribution. There looks like a wall for the acousticness too in 0 , so I chose to delete 0. [ Of course my decision to delete the values which looked capped to me is made according to the Aurelien Geron book ] As for the instrumentalness, I don't think there is a wall or that the data is capped, there is just a lot of singed songs in this datset which appears normal .And the duration of the songs in ms looks like it follows a gaussian distribution .As for others I am not seeing a clear distribution .
train = train[train['track_popularity'] != 0 ]
train = train[ train['acousticness'] != 0]
train[numerical_column].hist(bins = 50, figsize = (20,15))

### for the albums
we say it is useless for deep learning

#### here we can see some clusters formning at least 7, so  we can cluster the albums by their number of songs ! so we can have 8 clusters with each one having the same numbers of songs but the last one having more than 8 songs
we conduct the same approach for the playlist !

playlist_stats = train.groupby('playlist_id').agg(
    total_songs=('track_popularity', 'size'),
    mean_popularity=('track_popularity', 'mean')
).reset_index()
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.scatter(playlist_stats['total_songs'], playlist_stats['mean_popularity'], alpha=0.6)
plt.title('Total Songs vs Mean Popularity per Playlist')
plt.xlabel('Total Songs')
plt.ylabel('Mean Popularity')
plt.grid(True)
#plt.savefig("playits clusters 1 ")
plt.show()
Smaller playlists are more diverse in popularity and larger playlists tend to stabilize around moderate popularity levels.
There are somme outliers , we need to delete them

train = train.merge(playlist_stats[['playlist_id', 'total_songs']], on='playlist_id', how='left')


train = train[train['total_songs'] <= 125]
train = train.drop('total_songs', axis=1)

playlist_stats = train.groupby('playlist_id').agg(
    total_songs=('track_popularity', 'size'),
    mean_popularity=('track_popularity', 'mean')
).reset_index()
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.scatter(playlist_stats['total_songs'], playlist_stats['mean_popularity'], alpha=0.6)
plt.title('Total Songs vs Mean Popularity per Playlist')
plt.xlabel('Total Songs')
plt.ylabel('Mean Popularity')
plt.grid(True)
#plt.savefig("playits clusters 1 ")
plt.show()
it seems that there is one outliers in the variance popularity , but most of the songs have a small variance, let's use k-means to identify 3 clusters according to the total songs and mean popularity

playlist_stats = train.groupby('playlist_id').agg(
    mean_popularity=('track_popularity', 'mean'),
    total_songs=('track_popularity', 'size')
).reset_index()


kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=44)
playlist_stats['cluster'] = kmeans.fit_predict(playlist_stats[['total_songs', 'mean_popularity']])

train = train.merge(playlist_stats[['playlist_id', 'cluster']], on='playlist_id', how='left')

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.scatter(playlist_stats['total_songs'], playlist_stats['mean_popularity'], 
            c=playlist_stats['cluster'], cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
#joblib.dump(kmeans, 'kmeans_playlist_model.joblib')
# Step 4: Customize the plot
plt.title('KMeans Clustering: Total Songs vs Mean Popularity per Playlist')
plt.xlabel('Total Songs')
plt.ylabel('Mean Popularity')
plt.colorbar(label='Cluster')
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
joblib.dump(kmeans, 'kmeans_playlist_model_DL.joblib')

# 2/ Task 1 : Predict Popularity
### 2.1/ The Strategy

#### Well the strategy is not that complicated, we will use the embedding layers to transform the categorical features of the data "in parallel" so we use concatenate to concatanate the layers with the numercial data. Then we use a MLP with a ReLu activation ( we dont scale the objective) . I tried to implement a baysean approach as in the Machine Learning part but it was too complicated so I chose the best model mysel while "playing" with some hyperparameters

# Define input dimensions
embedding_dim = 32
output_dim = 1
#sh = (21012,)
sh = (1,)

# Updating input dimensions based on actual number of unique values
input_dims = {
    'track_name': len(encoders['track_name'].classes_),
    'track_artist': len(encoders['track_artist'].classes_),
    'track_album_name': len(encoders['track_album_name'].classes_),
    'playlist_name': len(encoders['playlist_name'].classes_),
    'playlist_genre': len(encoders['playlist_genre'].classes_),
    'playlist_subgenre': len(encoders['playlist_subgenre'].classes_),
    'artist_popularity_category': len(encoders['artist_popularity_category'].classes_)
}

# Input layers for each catagoric feature
track_name_input = Input(shape=sh, name='track_name_input')  
track_artist_input = Input(shape=sh, name='track_artist_input')  
track_album_name_input = Input(shape=sh, name='track_album_name_input')  
playlist_name_input = Input(shape=sh, name='playlist_name_input')  
playlist_genre_input = Input(shape=sh, name='playlist_genre_input')  
playlist_subgenre_input = Input(shape=sh, name='playlist_subgenre_input')  
artist_popularity_category_input = Input(shape=sh, name='artist_popularity_category_input') 

# Embedding layers for each feature
track_name_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['track_name'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(track_name_input)
track_artist_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['track_artist'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(track_artist_input)
track_album_name_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['track_album_name'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(track_album_name_input)
playlist_name_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['playlist_name'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(playlist_name_input)
playlist_genre_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['playlist_genre'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(playlist_genre_input)
playlist_subgenre_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['playlist_subgenre'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(playlist_subgenre_input)
artist_popularity_category_embed = Embedding(input_dim=input_dims['artist_popularity_category'], output_dim=embedding_dim)(artist_popularity_category_input)

# Flatten the embeddings
track_name_embed = Flatten()(track_name_embed)
track_artist_embed = Flatten()(track_artist_embed)
track_album_name_embed = Flatten()(track_album_name_embed)
playlist_name_embed = Flatten()(playlist_name_embed)
playlist_genre_embed = Flatten()(playlist_genre_embed)
playlist_subgenre_embed = Flatten()(playlist_subgenre_embed)
artist_popularity_category_embed = Flatten()(artist_popularity_category_embed)
# Input layers for each numeric feature

num_input = Input(shape=(11,), name='numeric')
#num_input = Flatten()(num_input)

# Concatenate the embeddings
merged = Concatenate()([track_name_embed, track_artist_embed, track_album_name_embed,
                        playlist_name_embed, playlist_genre_embed, playlist_subgenre_embed,
                        artist_popularity_category_embed,num_input ])

"""
x = Dense(64, activation='relu')(merged)
#x = Dropout(0.3)(x) 
x = Dense(32, activation='relu')(x)
#x = Dropout(0.3)(x) 
x = Dense(16, activation='relu')(x)
#x = Dropout(0.3)(x) 
x = Dense(8, activation='relu')(x)
#x = Dropout(0.3)(x) 
"""
x = Dense(128, activation='relu')(merged)
x = Dropout(0.3)(x)
x = BatchNormalization()(x)

x = Dense(64, activation='relu')(x)
x = Dropout(0.3)(x)
x = BatchNormalization()(x)

x = Dense(32, activation='relu')(x)
x = Dropout(0.2)(x)
# Output layer
output = Dense(output_dim, activation='relu')(x)

# Input layer
input = {
    'track_name': track_name_input,  
    'track_artist': track_artist_input,
    'track_album_name': track_album_name_input,
    'playlist_name': playlist_name_input,
    'playlist_genre': playlist_genre_input,
    'playlist_subgenre': playlist_subgenre_input,
    'artist_popularity_category': artist_popularity_category_input,
    'numeric': num_input
}
#Create the model
model = Model(inputs= input, outputs=output)
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.01)
# Compile the model
model.compile(optimizer=optimizer, loss='mean_squared_logarithmic_error', metrics=['mean_squared_logarithmic_error'])

# Summary of the model
model.summary()

callbacks = [
    ReduceLROnPlateau(
        monitor='val_loss',
        factor=0.5,
        patience=5,
        min_lr=1e-6,
        verbose=1
    ),
    EarlyStopping(
        monitor='val_loss',
        min_delta=0.05,
        patience=15,
        restore_best_weights=True,
        verbose=1
    )

y_train = train['track_popularity']
X_train = train.drop(columns='track_popularity')
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X_train, y_train, test_size=0.2, random_state=80)
early_stopping = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=12, restore_best_weights=True)

numerical_columns = ['danceability', 'energy', 'key',
                     'loudness','mode','speechiness','acousticness','instrumentalness',
                     'liveness','valence','tempo'] 


X_train_formatted = {
    'track_name': X_train['track_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_artist': X_train['track_artist'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_album_name': X_train['track_album_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_name': X_train['playlist_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_genre': X_train['playlist_genre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_subgenre': X_train['playlist_subgenre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'artist_popularity_category': X_train['artist_popularity_category'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'numeric': X_train[numerical_columns].values
}

X_val_formatted = {
    'track_name': X_val['track_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_artist': X_val['track_artist'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_album_name': X_val['track_album_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_name': X_val['playlist_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_genre': X_val['playlist_genre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_subgenre': X_val['playlist_subgenre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'artist_popularity_category': X_val['artist_popularity_category'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'numeric': X_val[numerical_columns].values
}

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder, MinMaxScaler
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.models import load_model
import joblib
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

categorical_columns = ['track_name', 'track_artist', 'track_album_name', 
                      'playlist_name', 'playlist_genre', 'playlist_subgenre', 
                      'artist_popularity_category']

numerical_columns = ['danceability', 'energy', 'key',
                    'loudness', 'mode', 'speechiness', 'acousticness',
                    'instrumentalness', 'liveness', 'valence', 'tempo']

def categorize_popularity(mean_pop):
    if mean_pop >= 60:
        return 'Popular'
    elif mean_pop >= 30:
        return 'Semi-popular'
    else:
        return 'Not Popular'

train = pd.read_csv("spotify_songs_train.csv")
artist_stats = train.groupby('track_artist').agg(
    mean_popularity=('track_popularity', 'mean')
).reset_index()
artist_stats['artist_popularity_category'] = artist_stats['mean_popularity'].apply(categorize_popularity)

artist_stats_train = dict(zip(artist_stats['track_artist'], 
                             artist_stats['artist_popularity_category']))

def classify_artist(artist):
    if artist in artist_stats_train:
        return artist_stats_train[artist]
    else:
        return 'Semi-popular'

test = pd.read_csv("spotify_songs_X_test.csv")

test = test.drop(['track_id', 'track_album_id', 'playlist_id', 'track_album_release_date'], axis=1)

test['artist_popularity_category'] = test['track_artist'].apply(classify_artist)

encoders = {}
for col in categorical_columns:
    le = LabelEncoder()
    test[col] = le.fit_transform(test[col])
    encoders[col] = le

scaler = MinMaxScaler()
test[numerical_columns] = scaler.fit_transform(test[numerical_columns])

X_test_formatted = {
    'track_name': test['track_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_artist': test['track_artist'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'track_album_name': test['track_album_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_name': test['playlist_name'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_genre': test['playlist_genre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'playlist_subgenre': test['playlist_subgenre'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'artist_popularity_category': test['artist_popularity_category'].values.reshape(-1, 1),
    'numeric': test[numerical_columns].values
}

model = joblib.load('HAXHIU_DL_model_6_best.joblib')
y_pred = model.predict(X_test_formatted)
y_pred
